title: Manual override mismatch in climate control
summary: The greenhouse control system reports one actuator state, but field indications suggest that a manual override may have changed the real physical state.
suspected_issue: A manual override may have desynchronized the reported actuator state from the real physical state in Sector B climate control.
symptoms: The digital system reports that climate actuators are operating normally.
Field observation suggests that at least one actuator may have been manually overridden.
Sector B climate conditions are not improving as expected from the reported actuator state.
The crop is in production phase and delayed correction could affect quality and disease exposure.

observed_signals.interpretation: Humidity remains too high despite the reported control state, suggesting a possible mismatch between digital and physical behavior.
Temperature looks stable, but this does not resolve the mismatch concern around actuator effectiveness.
Communication appears available, so the mismatch is more likely related to manual intervention than full telemetry loss.

operational_context.note: A recent manual intervention may have occurred in Sector B climate control.
operational_context.impact: The service should prioritize physical verification of actuator state instead of trusting digital status alone.

operational_context.note: No explicit backup climate-control path is confirmed for this mismatch scenario.
operational_context.impact: The service should not assume a clean fallback path and should emphasize review and verification.

agronomic_context.note: Persistent climate mismatch during production can sustain high humidity and increase disease pressure.
agronomic_context.impact: A delayed correction may affect both crop quality and plant-health stability.

business_context.note: A mismatch between reported state and real field behavior reduces trust in the control layer.
business_context.impact: The operational impact is not only technical but also managerial, because decisions become less reliable.

notes: This case is designed to test whether Agro-DO reacts prudently when the problem is not a direct failure or visibility outage, but a mismatch between the digital control state and probable physical reality.
